element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns available teams (24 from 1991-92 NHL season) and can customize lines before play
Single-game exhibition or playoff mode (single-elimination or best-of-7)
Player discovers scoring is accessible but requires strategy; AI difficulty noted as not challenging enough
Realistic mechanic that affects team depth and strategy decisions
Visual feedback and realism element that appears during aggressive play
Long-term progression goal; Stanley Cup-like trophy reward for winners

typical_tension_triggers: Screen overcrowding during fast play (noted in reviews)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Fast-paced real-time action with natural breaks between periods; tension builds through possession changes and scoring threats; relief through successful plays and match conclusions

difficulty_curve.early_game: Accessible—scoring described as 'too easy'; players can learn mechanics in exhibition mode without penalty
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Moderate challenge in playoff progression; best-of-7 series requires consistency; AI difficulty remains lower than desired per reviews
difficulty_curve.late_game: Long playoff runs (best-of-7) demand sustained performance; deeper roster strategy becomes relevant with injuries
notable_spikes: Playoff advancement when facing stronger teams

notable_level_patterns.description: Single one-off game between any two of 24 teams; low stakes, allows free experimentation and roster customization
Tournament structure where one loss ends progression; maximum tension with elimination stakes
Extended playoff series requiring up to 7 wins; builds tension across multiple matches; allows roster and strategy iteration between games
